summary: Surface a named 'query' MCP tool property for the search and corpus commands so an agent can call them directly, instead of having to smuggle the term through the generic 'args' string.
reason: The generated cobratree MCP mirror (printing-press 4.24.0) exposes only a generic 'args' string for a command's positional arguments. For search (Use: 'search <query>') and corpus (Use: 'corpus <query>') this means an MCP client cannot discover the command needs a query and must pass it through 'args' (finding N1; upstream problem report cli-printing-press#2517). Printing-press 4.27.1 fixes this generically (cli-printing-press#3332, 'preserve mcp mirror positional args'), but ships it welded into a larger cobratree rewrite (a new internal/mcp/bound output-bounding package, MCP argument-name validation, write-sink annotations, changed shellOutToCLI signature) that cannot be rendered by hand without a reprint and would clobber this fork's hand-added cookie-file MCP-block patch (medium-reader-mcp-block-cookie-file). So this fork ports only the narrow, low-risk slice: a per-command allowlist (search, corpus) that surfaces a required named 'query' string property and drops the generic 'args' property for those two commands only. Every other positional command (read <url|id>, feed <@user|publication|tag>, author-archive <user-id>, author-compare <a> <b>) keeps the proven 'args' fallback, avoiding pipe-style or ordered multi-token property names and the multi-positional ordering edge cases. The positional value is consumed out of a COPY of the MCP argument map BEFORE cliArgsFromMCP runs, so it is appended as a bare positional argv token and never re-emitted as --query (the double-emit trap); flag-like positional values are rejected exactly like the existing 'args'-field guard. The cookie-file MCP-block patch is untouched (cliArgsFromMCP, blockedRootFlags and toolOptionsForFlags are unchanged), and old MCP clients that still pass the query via 'args' keep working because the raw 'args' fallback still fires.
validated_outcome: go build ./... and go test ./internal/mcp/cobratree/ green; gofmt and go vet clean. Unit tests assert search/corpus surface a required 'query' property and that the value is appended as a bare positional token, never as --query, with flag-like values rejected. An in-process integration test (TestRegisterAll_NarrowNamedPositionals) drives the real RegisterAll walker and inspects the registered tool schemas via the mcp-go ListTools accessor: search and corpus advertise a required 'query' and no 'args', while read (outside the allowlist) keeps 'args' and gains no 'query'. The existing cookie-file MCP-block regression tests (TestCliArgsFromMCP_BlocksRootFlags, TestToolOptionsForFlags_OmitsBlockedRootFlags) remain green, confirming no conflict with that patch. Full live MCP tools/list parity against the registered 'medium-reader' server is scheduled for a fresh session per specs/test-protocol.md Phase 5 (the server must reload).

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Generic named-positional MCP surface for every command (the printing-press >=4.27.x cobratree rewrite).
deferred_to_upstream.reason: The full generic fix ships upstream but is entangled with the bound/validation cobratree rewrite; this narrow hand-port covers only search/corpus. A future reprint onto the newer generator supersedes this patch with the generic behavior for all commands.
